name: Kitchen Renovation Planner
description: Plan your kitchen renovation end to end. Compare countertop and cabinet options, budget by category, and build a complete shopping list.
metaDescription: Free kitchen renovation planner. Compare quartz, granite, and laminate countertops. Estimate budget, timeline, and materials. Interactive planning tool.

scopeTiers.name: Cosmetic Refresh
scopeTiers.description: New cabinet fronts, countertops, backsplash, hardware, and appliances. Keep existing layout.
includes: Replace cabinet doors and drawer fronts

scopeTiers.name: Mid-Range Renovation
scopeTiers.description: New cabinets, countertops, flooring, and appliances. Minor layout changes.

scopeTiers.name: Full Gut Renovation
scopeTiers.description: Strip to studs. Relocate plumbing, gas, and electrical. Custom cabinetry and premium finishes.

qualityTiers.name: Budget-Friendly
qualityTiers.description: Smart choices that maximize value. Laminate counters, stock cabinets, and mid-tier appliances.

qualityTiers.name: Mid-Range
qualityTiers.description: Quality materials with great durability and style. Quartz counters, semi-custom cabinets, stainless appliances.

qualityTiers.name: Premium
qualityTiers.description: Designer finishes throughout. Custom cabinetry, natural stone, professional-grade appliances.

options.name: Laminate
options.description: Budget-friendly. Many patterns and colors. Easy to fabricate.

options.name: Butcher Block
options.description: Warm, natural wood surface. Can be sanded and refinished. Needs oiling.

options.name: Quartz
options.description: Engineered stone. Non-porous, stain-resistant. Most popular choice.

options.name: Granite
options.description: Natural stone. Heat resistant. Unique veining. Needs annual sealing.

options.name: Marble
options.description: Luxury natural stone. Etches from acids (lemon, vinegar). High maintenance.

options.name: Porcelain Slab
options.description: Large-format porcelain. Ultra-durable. UV-stable. No sealing needed.

options.name: Stock Cabinets
options.description: Pre-built standard sizes. Limited styles and finishes. Most affordable.

options.name: Semi-Custom Cabinets
options.description: Choose size, finish, door style, and interior options. Best value.

options.name: Custom Cabinets
options.description: Built to exact specifications. Any wood, finish, or configuration possible.

options.name: Porcelain Tile
options.description: Extremely durable. Waterproof. Great for high-traffic kitchens.

options.name: Engineered Hardwood
options.description: Real wood veneer. Stable in moisture. Can be refinished 1-2 times.

options.name: Natural Stone
options.description: Marble, travertine, or limestone. Unique. Requires sealing.

options.name: Subway Tile
options.description: Classic 3x6 ceramic. Timeless, affordable, easy to install.

options.name: Mosaic Tile
options.description: Small tiles on mesh sheets. Intricate patterns. Requires skilled install.

options.name: Quartz Slab Backsplash
options.description: Seamless full-height slab. No grout lines. Matches countertop perfectly.

budgetCategories.name: Cabinetry & Hardware
budgetCategories.description: Cabinets, drawer boxes, doors, hardware, and installation.

budgetCategories.name: Countertop & Backsplash
budgetCategories.description: Countertop material, fabrication, backsplash, and installation.

budgetCategories.name: Appliances
budgetCategories.description: Range, refrigerator, dishwasher, microwave, vent hood.

budgetCategories.name: Plumbing
budgetCategories.description: Sink, faucet, garbage disposal, water line, drain rough-in.

budgetCategories.name: Electrical & Lighting
budgetCategories.description: Rewiring, outlets, switches, under-cabinet lights, pendants.

budgetCategories.name: Demolition & Disposal
budgetCategories.description: Remove old cabinets, demo floors, debris haul-away.

budgetCategories.name: Walls & Ceiling
budgetCategories.description: Drywall repair, paint, crown molding, trim.

budgetCategories.name: Permits & Inspections
budgetCategories.description: Building permits, gas permit, electrical inspection.

budgetCategories.name: Contingency
budgetCategories.description: Unexpected issues: rotten subfloor, outdated wiring, gas line changes.

timelinePhases.name: Planning & Design
timelinePhases.description: Layout design, material selection, permits, ordering.
details: Decide on layout (L-shape, galley, U-shape, island)
Order cabinets (8-12 week lead time typical)

timelinePhases.name: Preparation
timelinePhases.description: Set up temporary kitchen, protect the home, disconnect services.
details: Set up temporary kitchen (microwave, hot plate, mini-fridge)
Protect floors and furniture in adjoining rooms
Turn off water and gas to kitchen

timelinePhases.name: Demolition
timelinePhases.description: Remove old cabinets, counters, flooring, and appliances.

timelinePhases.name: Rough-In Work
timelinePhases.description: Plumbing, electrical, gas lines before walls are closed.
details: Relocate gas line if moving range

timelinePhases.name: Walls & Flooring
timelinePhases.description: Prepare and finish walls and floors before cabinets go in.
details: Prime and paint walls and ceiling
Let flooring acclimate per manufacturer specs

timelinePhases.name: Countertop & Backsplash
timelinePhases.description: Template, fabricate, and install countertop and backsplash.

timelinePhases.name: Finishing Touches
details: Caulk countertop seams and backsplash edges

faqs.q: How long does a kitchen renovation take?
faqs.a: A cosmetic refresh typically takes 1-2 weeks. A mid-range renovation takes 4-8 weeks. A full gut renovation with custom cabinets and layout changes can take 8-16 weeks, largely due to cabinet fabrication lead times.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for kitchen renovation?
faqs.a: Permits are typically required when you change the kitchen layout, relocate plumbing or gas, add electrical circuits, or alter load-bearing walls. Cosmetic updates (countertops, backsplash, painting) usually don't need permits. Always check with your local building department.

faqs.q: What is the most expensive part of a kitchen renovation?
faqs.a: Cabinetry is the single largest expense (25-35% of total budget). Appliances are next (15-25%), followed by countertops and labor. Smart money-saving strategy: keep the same layout to avoid costly plumbing and gas relocations.

faqs.q: Should I replace cabinets or reface them?
faqs.a: Refacing (new doors and drawer fronts) costs 30-50% less than full replacement and works well if the cabinet boxes are solid wood or plywood in good condition. Replace if the layout doesn't work, the boxes are particle board, or you want different cabinet sizes.

faqs.q: What is the best kitchen countertop material?
faqs.a: Quartz is the most popular choice (60% of new kitchens) for its balance of durability, low maintenance, and appearance. Granite offers natural beauty at a similar price. Laminate is best for tight budgets. Marble is for design-forward homeowners who don't mind maintenance.

faqs.q: Can I DIY a kitchen renovation?
faqs.a: Cabinet installation and painting are DIY-friendly. Countertop fabrication, plumbing, gas, and electrical work should be done by licensed professionals. A common compromise: demo the old kitchen yourself, then hire pros for rough-in and countertops.

faqs.q: How much contingency should I budget for a kitchen?
faqs.a: 15-20% is standard. Common surprises include: subfloor rot discovered after demo, outdated electrical panels, galvanized pipe replacement, and gas line relocation. Opening walls during demo is where most surprises are found.

faqs.q: What adds the most value to a kitchen renovation?
faqs.a: Mid-range kitchens recoup 55-65% of cost at resale. Top value drivers: quality cabinets, quartz or granite countertops, stainless steel appliances, and good lighting. Avoid over-building for the neighborhood and highly personalized design choices.
